scenarios.title: AI Compute Orchestration
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when a task mentions SkyPilot, sky CLI, task YAMLs, cloud/Kubernetes/Slurm compute, GPUs/TPUs, clusters, managed jobs, SkyServe, storage mounts, or cross-cloud AI workload orchestration.
scenarios.role: Routes SkyPilot end-user workflows across task YAML authoring, interactive clusters, managed jobs, serving, infrastructure/storage, and the Python SDK.
scenarios.read_when: SkyPilot, skypilot, sky launch, sky exec, sky jobs, sky serve, sky check, sky gpus, task.yaml, SkyServe, managed spot, Kubernetes GPUs, Slurm, file_mounts, volumes, cloud failover, GPU availability, API server.
scenarios.best_for: Writing SkyPilot YAMLs, planning safe CLI/SDK commands, debugging SkyPilot install/API server/cloud/provider issues, deploying managed jobs or services, and translating AI workload requirements into SkyPilot workflows.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is local-only Docker/conda work with no SkyPilot involvement, asks for a generic cloud provider SDK instead of SkyPilot, or needs direct credentials/resource mutation without user authorization.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `skypilot` when SkyPilot is the orchestration layer; route to a sub-skill by practical user goal rather than source module name. Prefer task-yaml before cluster/jobs/serve when the user provides a YAML or config snippet.

scenarios.title: Repository Development
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when changing SkyPilot source code, tests, docs, dashboard, API server schemas, protobufs, dependencies, CI, or PR validation plans.
scenarios.role: Provides SkyPilot-specific maintainer guidance for coding conventions, risky code paths, focused tests, formatting, protobuf/dashboard regeneration, API compatibility, and PR handoff.
scenarios.read_when: SkyPilot repo, AGENTS.md, format.sh, pytest SkyPilot, API_VERSION, protobuf regeneration, dashboard build, managed jobs recovery, API server performance, SkyPilot PR Tested section.
scenarios.best_for: Editing SkyPilot code safely, selecting focused unit/smoke tests, avoiding generated-file mistakes, and preparing compatibility-aware code review notes.
scenarios.avoid_when: The user only wants to run workloads with SkyPilot rather than modify the SkyPilot repository; use the end-user scenario instead.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose repo-development only for source-maintenance tasks; choose ai-compute-orchestration for normal SkyPilot usage.
